expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (check_threads, lock, performance_schema, RippleParameters).
expectations.evidence: All required substrings present multiple times.

expectations.text: Response does not contain forbidden substring `the fix is to restart the kernel`.
expectations.evidence: Forbidden substring absent. Response actively warns 'A restart-the-kernel-and-try-again instinct doesn't help here'.

expectations.text: Routes to check_threads(detailed=True) BEFORE suggesting a kernel restart
expectations.evidence: Section 'Diagnose with check_threads(detailed=True)' shown as the primary diagnostic at helpers.py:206; explicit 'don't restart the kernel without a diagnostic first'.

expectations.text: Distinguishes idle hang from slow-compute, picks lock contention for params table
expectations.evidence: 'Distinguishing idle hang from slow compute' section: 'Slow compute would show CPU activity ... Lock contention (idle hang) — no CPU, no I/O ... Params tables are never the slow-compute case'.

expectations.text: Names performance_schema.metadata_locks (joined with threads); user reads LOCK_STATUS/PROCESSLIST_STATE
expectations.evidence: 'queries MySQL's performance_schema (joining performance_schema.metadata_locks (ml) with performance_schema.threads (t))'; explicit columns named (LOCK_STATUS GRANTED vs WAITING, PROCESSLIST_STATE) and explicit 'output is lock rows + thread state, NOT an explicit blocker→waiter graph'.

expectations.text: Recommends coordinating with the lab before killing
expectations.evidence: 'Resolution — coordinate, don't unilaterally KILL': 4-step workflow that ends with 'Only after they're aware/agreed: an admin can issue KILL'.
